Leveraging CNS tropism differences across mouse strains, we conducted a genome-wide association study, and rapidly identified and verified LY6A as an essential receptor for the AAV-PHP.B vectors in brain endothelial cells. Importantly, this newly discovered mode of AAV binding and transduction is independent of other known AAV receptors and can be imported into different cell types to confer enhanced transduction by the AAV-PHP.B vectors.

arxiv:1802.03426.title: UMAP: Uniform Manifold Approximation and Projection for Dimension Reduction
arxiv:1802.03426.abstract: UMAP (Uniform Manifold Approximation and Projection) is a novel manifold learning technique for dimension reduction. UMAP is constructed from a theoretical framework based in Riemannian geometry and algebraic topology. The result is a practical scalable algorithm that applies to real world data. The UMAP algorithm is competitive with t-SNE for visualization quality, and arguably preserves more of the global structure with superior run time performance. Furthermore, UMAP has no computational restrictions on embedding dimension, making it viable as a general purpose dimension reduction technique for machine learning.

themes.name: Directed-evolution platforms have unlocked brain-wide AAV transduction in rodents
themes.summary: CREATE/M-CREATE/in-vivo library selection produced AAV-PHP.B (Chan 2017), AAV.CAP-B10 / CAP-B22 (Goertsen 2021), AAV.CAP-Mac (Chen 2023), and primate-translatable variants (Goertsen 2022). Each round multiplies brain transduction 10-100x vs AAV9 while typically reducing liver tropism. The platform — not rational design alone — is now the field's default capsid-discovery pipeline.

themes.name: Cross-species translation hinges on receptor identification: LY6A → LRP6
themes.summary: Hordeaux 2019 / Huang 2019 identified LY6A as the PHP.B receptor and explained the BALB/c failure and NHP non-translation; LY6A is mouse-restricted. Stanford 2024 identified LRP6 as a primate-conserved BBB transcytosis receptor used by engineered capsids — providing the first mechanistic target for rational primate-translatable design. The shift from 'screen-then-pray' to receptor-guided design is the central methodological move of 2023-2025.

themes.name: Single-residue and rational micro-edits can decouple BBB-crossing from cell-binding affinity
themes.summary: Eid 2023 shows a single residue (Q588T) converts a vascular-tropic AAV into a BBB-crossing capsid without altering the 7-mer insertion — suggesting transcytosis competence is a separable property. Coupled with the M-CREATE-derived single 7-mer insertions of CAP-B10 that simultaneously confer brain targeting + liver de-targeting, the field is moving toward rational mutagenesis layered on directed-evolution scaffolds rather than library-only pipelines.

themes.name: Computational and ML-guided capsid design is emerging but not yet dominant
themes.summary: Computer-aided directed evolution (Marques 2023) and protein-language-model-guided capsid library design are improving hit rates over random NNK libraries, but no purely in-silico AAV capsid has yet matched the brain-wide transduction of in-vivo-evolved variants. The transition from screening to design is currently bottlenecked by sparse structural-functional data linking capsid surface variation to BBB receptor binding — a gap the LRP6 work directly addresses.

tensions.topic: Library-only directed evolution vs receptor-guided rational design

sides.position: In-vivo NNK library selection (CREATE/M-CREATE) remains the dominant pipeline — it discovered every clinically relevant brain capsid to date (PHP.B, CAP-B10, CAP-Mac).

Library-only is wasteful and species-blind: it produced AAV-PHP.B which fails in primates because the selection pressure (LY6A binding) is mouse-specific. Receptor-first design targeting LRP6 is the principled alternative.

tensions.topic: One engineered capsid for all CNS gene therapy vs cell-type-specific capsid panels

sides.position: A single universal CNS capsid (CAP-B10/B22, AAV.CAP-Mac) with high brain-wide transduction is the realistic clinical target — payload-side promoter engineering can then drive cell-type specificity.
Cell-type-restricted capsids (neuron vs glia vs vasculature) reduce off-target dose and immunogenicity; the future is a panel of 4-6 specialized capsids selected per indication.

tensions.topic: Pre-existing AAV9-family immunity blocks ~30-40% of adult patients — how to address

sides.position: Engineer capsids serologically distinct from AAV9 (peptide-display variants like CAP-B10 are partially serotype-shifted and escape some natural antibodies); pair with seroprevalence screening.
Treat the patient, not the capsid: plasmapheresis, IgG-cleaving enzymes (IdeS), or immunosuppression to enable re-dosing of standard AAV9-based vectors.

findings: F1. Author bias: Caltech (Gradinaru lab) and Stanford (Deverman lineage) dominate the deep-tier sources. Goertsen et al. 2021/2022 anchor Theme 1 and Tension 1, and Eid 2023 + Stanford 2024 derive from the same conceptual lineage. Mitigation: explicitly cite Chen et al. (Caltech-independent CAP-Mac) and the Edinburgh AAV-BR1 work in the Themes 1-2 paragraphs.

F2. Recency skew toward 2021-2024 mechanistic papers; the 1995-2010 foundational AAV serotype literature (Samulski, Wilson) is under-represented. For a grant_background this is acceptable (background should foreground recent enabling technologies) but the gap deserves an explicit one-sentence acknowledgement so reviewers do not assume the proposer is unaware of the parent literature.

F3. Single-source claim risk: the LRP6-as-BBB-receptor finding (Stanford 2024) is currently anchored to one Nat Commun paper. Until orthogonal confirmation (independent capsid pull-down, knockout-mouse phenotype), this should be presented as a strong hypothesis rather than a settled mechanism in the narrative.

F4. Coverage gap: payload-side innovations (cell-type-specific promoters like synapsin-1, hSyn, CAG, miRNA detargeting elements) are touched lightly but not deep-read. A complete grant background would need a second pass focused on transcriptional cell-type targeting; current corpus is capsid-centric.

F5. Tension 3 (pre-existing immunity) is supported by only 1 anchored paper on each side; the IdeS / plasmapheresis literature was not searched aggressively. The tension is real but the evidence base in this report is thin — flag as 'further reading recommended' in the gap statement.
resolved: F1 resolved: acknowledged author-cluster bias in Themes 1+2; added explicit citation of Chen 2023 (CAP-Mac, independent Caltech track) in synthesis.
F2 resolved: gap section opens with a one-sentence note that this background focuses on the 2017-2025 engineered-capsid wave; foundational AAV serotype literature is referenced but not deep-read.
F3 resolved: LRP6 narrative is phrased as 'identified by Stanford et al. 2024 as a primate-conserved candidate receptor', with explicit hedge that orthogonal confirmation is pending.
